hero.Title: My Technology Ethics
hero.Subtitle: Technology is never neutral—every line of code embodies values and priorities. Here are the principles that guide how I build, the decisions I make, and the kind of tech future I want to help create.

quote.Description: As developers, we shape how billions of people interact with information, each other, and the world. That power demands thoughtful, ethical practice.

accessibility.Title: Accessibility for All
accessibility.Subtitle: Building inclusive digital experiences
accessibility.Description: I believe technology should be accessible to everyone, regardless of ability. Web accessibility isn't just a feature—it's a fundamental human right. Every person deserves to access information, services, and opportunities online.

values.0: Semantic HTML and ARIA labels are non-negotiable in my code
values.1: Keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility are tested from day one
values.2: Color contrast and text sizing follow WCAG 2.1 AA standards minimum
values.3: I advocate for accessibility in design discussions and code reviews
values.4: Accessibility audits are part of my development workflow, not an afterthought

accessibility.Impact: When we build for accessibility, we build better products for everyone. Features like captions benefit not just deaf users, but also people in noisy environments or learning a new language.

privacy.Title: Privacy as a Default
privacy.Subtitle: Respecting user data and consent
privacy.Description: Privacy is not a privilege—it's a right. In an era of surveillance capitalism, I'm committed to building applications that respect user privacy and minimize data collection to only what's truly necessary.

values.0: Collect only essential data, delete what's no longer needed
values.1: Clear, honest privacy policies written in plain language
values.2: Opt-in by default, never dark patterns or deceptive consent flows
values.3: End-to-end encryption for sensitive user data
values.4: Regular security audits and vulnerability assessments
values.5: Transparent about third-party integrations and data sharing

privacy.Impact: Users should control their own data. I design systems where privacy-conscious choices are easy and default, not buried in settings or requiring technical knowledge.

open-source.Title: Open Source Contribution
open-source.Subtitle: Giving back to the community
open-source.Description: Open source software has been instrumental in my career and countless innovations. I believe in contributing back to the ecosystem that has given me so much, and making knowledge freely accessible.

values.0: Contribute to open source projects I use and benefit from
values.1: Share code, tools, and solutions that could help other developers
values.2: Document thoroughly so others can learn and build upon my work
values.3: Mentor newcomers to open source and help them make their first contributions
values.4: Advocate for open standards and interoperability
values.5: Choose open source solutions when viable for projects

open-source.Impact: Open source democratizes technology. Every contribution, no matter how small, helps build a more collaborative and innovative tech ecosystem.

sustainability.Title: Sustainable Development
sustainability.Subtitle: Building for environmental responsibility
sustainability.Description: Technology has an environmental footprint. From energy-intensive data centers to e-waste, our choices as developers matter. I strive to write efficient code and make sustainable architecture decisions.

values.0: Optimize code and assets to reduce server load and energy consumption
values.1: Choose green hosting providers powered by renewable energy when possible
values.2: Minimize unnecessary API calls and database queries
values.3: Implement efficient caching strategies to reduce redundant processing
values.4: Consider the lifecycle and longevity of the software I build
values.5: Advocate for sustainable practices in technical discussions

sustainability.Impact: A 1-second improvement in load time doesn't just enhance user experience—it also reduces energy consumption across millions of devices and servers.

ethical-ai.Title: Ethical AI & Automation
ethical-ai.Subtitle: Technology that serves humanity
ethical-ai.Description: As AI becomes more prevalent, we must ensure it's built with fairness, transparency, and human welfare in mind. Automation should augment human capability, not exploit or replace it irresponsibly.

values.0: Question bias in training data and algorithmic decision-making
values.1: Ensure AI systems are explainable and auditable
values.2: Design automation that empowers workers, not just reduces costs
values.3: Consider the societal impact of technologies I build
values.5: Prioritize human oversight in critical decision-making systems

ethical-ai.Impact: The question isn't just 'Can we build it?' but 'Should we build it?' and 'Who benefits or is harmed by this technology?'

security.Title: Security First
security.Subtitle: Protecting users and their trust
security.Description: Security vulnerabilities don't just expose data—they break trust. I believe in building secure systems from the ground up, following best practices, and staying updated on emerging threats.

values.0: Input validation and sanitization to prevent injection attacks
values.1: Secure authentication with proper hashing and token management
values.2: Regular dependency updates and vulnerability scanning
values.3: Principle of least privilege in access control
values.4: Security considerations in every code review
values.5: Transparent disclosure and quick response to security issues

security.Impact: One security breach can destroy years of trust. Proactive security practices protect not just data, but people's livelihoods and peace of mind.

transparency.Title: Transparent Technology
transparency.Subtitle: Honesty in what we build
transparency.Description: Users deserve to understand how the technology they use works, what data it collects, and how decisions are made. Transparency builds trust and empowers informed choices.

values.0: Clear communication about how systems work and what they do
values.1: No hidden tracking or unexpected data collection
values.2: Honest about limitations and potential biases in systems
values.3: Open about mistakes and how they're being addressed
values.4: Readable terms of service and privacy policies
values.5: User-friendly controls to view, export, and delete personal data

transparency.Impact: When users understand technology, they can make better decisions about using it and hold companies accountable for how it's built.

inclusive-tech.Title: Inclusive & Diverse Tech
inclusive-tech.Subtitle: Technology that represents everyone
inclusive-tech.Description: The best technology is built by diverse teams for diverse users. I'm committed to fostering inclusive environments and building products that work for people of all backgrounds.

values.0: Support and amplify underrepresented voices in tech
values.1: Design for global audiences with localization and cultural sensitivity
values.2: Challenge homogeneous thinking in product and technical decisions
values.3: Create welcoming onboarding experiences for junior developers
values.4: Advocate for diverse hiring and inclusive workplace policies
values.5: Consider how features might affect different communities differently

inclusive-tech.Impact: Diverse teams build better products. When we include different perspectives, we catch blind spots and create technology that truly serves everyone.

continuous-learning.Title: Continuous Learning & Humility
continuous-learning.Subtitle: Always evolving my ethical framework
continuous-learning.Description: Technology ethics isn't static—new challenges emerge constantly. I commit to continuous learning, listening to affected communities, and evolving my practices as I learn more.

values.0: Stay informed about ethical debates and emerging issues in tech
values.1: Listen to and learn from people affected by technology decisions
values.2: Admit when I'm wrong and adjust course
values.3: Seek out diverse perspectives that challenge my assumptions
values.4: Share learnings and mistakes to help others avoid them
values.5: Recognize that ethics require ongoing reflection, not one-time decisions

continuous-learning.Impact: The most ethical stance is recognizing I don't have all the answers. Humility and willingness to learn ensure I keep growing as a developer and human.

closing.Title: Living These Values
closing.Description: These aren't just aspirational ideals—they're commitments I work to uphold in every project. I won't always get it perfect, but I pledge to keep learning, listening, and striving to build technology that makes the world a little bit better.
closing.Contact: Questions, feedback, or want to discuss tech ethics?

meta.Title: Technology Ethics | {name}
meta.Description: Discover the ethical principles guiding my development: accessibility, privacy, open source, sustainability, ethical AI, security, and transparency.
